message:
🌱 Add flowise: [BUG] RecordManager Does Not Properly Cleanup Vector mission (#986)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "flowise-3570-bug-recordmanager-does-not-properly-cleanup-vector-store-s-old-valu",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "flowise: [BUG] RecordManager Does Not Properly Cleanup Vector Store's OLD VALUES",
+    "description": "[BUG] RecordManager Does Not Properly Cleanup Vector Store's OLD VALUES. Community-reported issue.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify flowise troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your flowise deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flowise -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise -n flowise --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review flowise configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant flowise configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flowise -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise
kubectl get configmap -n flowise -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flowise
```
In Flowise, I've created a new `Document Store` where an `Airtable` Document Loader feeds data into a Postgres Vector Store. We're also using Postgres to store the Record Manager records."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [BUG] RecordManager Does Not Properly Cleanup Vector…",
+        "description": "Okay, from this commit, I think I see why it was disabled:
https://github.com/FlowiseAI/Flowise/commit/a2a475ba7a1186ceec850cebe135426d2d822561#diff-4a1a4686f399cfa5448e01ad9b44f53e26ff79e5438d8748ae77771f25ac4c7eR2

This comment explains why in v2.2.5:
```
Temporary disabled due to increasing open
```yaml
flowise-data=> \d mechanisms_vec_v1
                 Table "public.mechanisms_vec_v1"
   Column    |  Type  | Collation | Nullable |      Default
-------------+--------+-----------+----------+--------------------
 id          | uuid   |           | not null | uuid_generate_v4()
 pageContent | text   |           |          |
 metadata    | jsonb  |           |          |
 embedding   | vector |           |          |
Indexes:
    "mechanisms_vec_v1_pkey" PRIMARY KEY, btree (id)

flowise-data=> \d mechanisms_rm_v1
                     Table "public.mechanisms_rm_v1"
   Column   |       Type     
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm [BUG] RecordManager Does Not Properly Cleanup… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise -n flowise --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n flowise --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Okay, from this commit, I think I see why it was disabled:
https://github.com/FlowiseAI/Flowise/commit/a2a475ba7a1186ceec850cebe135426d2d822561#diff-4a1a4686f399cfa5448e01ad9b44f53e26ff79e5438d8748ae77771f25ac4c7eR2

This comment explains why in v2.2.5:
```
Temporary disabled due to increasing open connections without releasing them
Use TypeORM instead
```

So while the workaround to use",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "flowise-data=> \d mechanisms_vec_v1
                 Table "public.mechanisms_vec_v1"
   Column    |  Type  | Collation | Nullable |      Default
-------------+--------+-----------+----------+--------------------
 id          | uuid   |           | not null | uuid_generate_v4()
 pageContent | text   |           |          |
 metadata    | jsonb  |           |          |
 embedding   | vector |           |          |
Indexes:
    "mechanisms_vec_v1_pkey" PRIMARY KEY, btree (id)

flowise-data=> \d mechanisms_rm_v1
                     Table "public.mechanisms_rm_v1"
   Column   |       Type       | Collation | Nullable |      Default
------------+------------------+-----------+----------+-------------------
 uuid       | uuid             |           | not null | gen_random_uuid()
 key       ",
+        "Instead, we can change it to:",
+        "### Implementation Notes for Maintainers:
1. Dependencies added:
   - `ioredis` for Redis operations
   - `redlock` for distributed locking
   - `xxhash-wasm` and `murmurhash3js-revisited` for efficient hashing

2. Configuration requirements:
   - Redis URL must be provided when Bloom filters are enabled
   - Namespace is derived from vector store configuration
   - Memory usage scales with expected document count and false positive rate

3. Backward compatibility:
   - All new features are opt-in via `useBloomFilter` option
   - Existing functionality remains unchanged when Bloom filters are disabled
   - No breaking changes to existing API

4. Performance characteristics:
   - Redis operations are batched to reduce network overhead
   - Bloom filter parameters auto-adjust based on docume"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
